signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.94 — Middle East tensions following Hormuz traffic reports revive USD safe-haven demand, while the +275bp Fed-BoJ carry (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0%) sustains the structural bid. JPY COT LF net short -137,828 (long% 25.3%) remains crowded, while USDJPY IV at 7.2% vs HV30 3.3% (VRP +3.9%) indicates the options market is pricing a significant premium for protection. 25d RR at -1.52 confirms a strong put skew as traders hedge against BoJ intervention or a sharp carry unwind. Carry dominates the regime while VIX remains at 15.8 (normal volatility), but intervention risk is acute above 162.00.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5685 — COT LF net short -25,757 (long% 19.5%) shows specs are heavily positioned for NZD weakness, yet OIS markets price a hike bias for the RBNZ meeting on 8 July. This creates a high-conviction short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers a hike or hawkish guidance. NZD real rate at -0.83% remains the most accommodative in the G10, providing a fundamental anchor for the spec short despite the OIS pricing. Watch for the RBNZ decision to trigger a crowded spec unwind toward 0.5800.

signals.title: AUD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/CHF at 0.5598 — G10 scorecard identifies a bilateral theme with a 0.806% spread between leader AUD and laggard CHF. The divergence is fundamentally supported by a massive +435bp carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs SNB 0.0%). AUD COT LF net long +21,597 (62.1%) aligns with the outperformance, while CHF net short -11,011 reflects the funding currency status. The theme persists as long as cross-asset volatility remains contained; a VIX spike above 20 would threaten this carry-driven outperformance.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4196 — CAD COT LF net short -92,861 (long% 20.6%) shows extreme institutional bearishness, while retail sentiment is 85% short, creating a rare institutional vs retail alignment that often precedes trend exhaustion. USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation at -0.881 (z=-2.04σ) indicates a significant break from the historical norm (+0.010), with CAD failing to benefit from the +2.87% intraday rally in WTI ($70.5). USD/CAD upside is structurally supported by a +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%), but the correlation break and crowded positioning suggest a move toward 1.4300 will face heavy supply.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Vol Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1423 — IV at 7.1% vs HV30 4.7% (VRP +2.4%) signals elevated hedging demand despite a near-neutral 25d RR of -0.29. The pair faces fundamental headwinds as the Economic Surprise Index (CESI) shows a strong negative bias for EUR (net -10) vs a strong positive bias for USD (net +282). The +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.25%) favors USD, keeping EUR/USD upside capped at 1.1500. Watch the ECB meeting on 23 July for any shift in the hiking stance to counter the persistent data misses.
